patterns.name: Never narrate actions â€” execute or stay silent
patterns.pattern: Never post a message describing what you're about to do (react, respond, wait, etc.). Either execute the action, or say nothing. This applies equally to: emoji reactions, task acknowledgments, and status updates. Posting 'react only' instead of actually reacting, or sending 'react' as text instead of calling the API â€” both fail the same way. Internal reasoning must stay internal.

patterns.name: Emoji reactions require react.ps1, not slack-notify.ps1
patterns.pattern: slack-notify.ps1 sends text messages only. For emoji reactions, always use react.ps1 (C:\Users\azureuser\shared\scripts\react.ps1) or the Slack API directly. Attempting to 'react' via slack-notify.ps1 sends the literal word 'react' as a message â€” a visible mistake.
